sectionOrderRules: Hero must state the business type and what is produced, processed or supplied, with a primary contact CTA.
Production summary must be concrete about materials, product types or manufacturing focus rather than generic industrial wording.
Trust proof must rely on real signals such as experience, facility information, delivery capability, visible company details or named references when available.
Facility overview, product highlights and process highlights may appear before trust proof when supported by source data.
Sections should avoid repeating the same claims about quality, precision or reliability.
Products intro must explain what categories, materials or formats the company offers.
Product list items must be specific, for example material groups, semi-finished products, dimensions, finishing options or customer-adapted production.
Do not imply stock levels, delivery times or standards unless such details are actually provided.
Processes intro must connect the company's production setup to customer needs such as repeatability, volume, tolerance or further processing.
Process list must describe real manufacturing or processing steps and not generic business services.
Workflow should show a plausible sequence such as inquiry, specification, production, control and delivery without inventing guarantees.
About story must describe the company, its production focus or history in specific terms.
Facility overview should focus on plant, equipment environment or way of working if data exists.
Team is optional in rendering if no staff data is available, but the route structure should support it.
Contact info must include real available contact paths such as phone, email, address or contact form.
If map or visit information is shown, it should help visitors find the site or understand how visits and goods reception work.
No placeholder contact details may be used.
